pi_items.source: Task description and export_result.sh (line 57-58) reference; verifier.py expects this location name

Setup_task.sh creates product.category with name 'Corrosives' (line 52-56); task description specifies routing to Corrosives category; verifier checks for category_id in putaway rule verification (line 69)
Setup_task.sh creates product with name 'Sulfuric Acid 98%' (line 64); task description mentions creating receipt for 'Sulfuric Acid 98%'; export_result.sh searches for this product (line 84)
Setup_task.sh creates res.partner (vendor) with name 'Chemical Suppliers Inc' (line 81-86); task description specifies receipt from 'Chemical Suppliers Inc'
Task description (line 5) specifies 'create a receipt for 5 units of Sulfuric Acid 98%'; export_result.sh retrieves qty_done from stock.move.line but does not explicitly validate the 5-unit quantity

privileged_info_summary: The task involves configuring an Odoo warehouse for hazardous material storage using synthetic test data. All five metadata items are verified as accurate: (1) Safety Cabinet 01 is the target location to be created; (2) Corrosives is the target product category, which correctly classifies Sulfuric Acid 98% (a real industrial chemical classified as corrosive/hazmat Class 8); (3) Sulfuric Acid 98% is the product to be routed, matching the standard industrial concentration of concentrated sulfuric acid; (4) Chemical Suppliers Inc is the vendor; (5) quantity 5 represents the receipt line item quantity in units. All metadata values are consistently referenced across the task description, setup script, export verification script, and verifier logic. The data is entirely synthetic (created at task runtime via setup_task.sh using Odoo XML-RPC) rather than sourced from external datasets.
